item.title: AI punters lose their shirts on Premier League bets
item.description: Models by Google, OpenAI, Anthropic and xAI struggle when asked to predict scores over football season

item.title: BP’s Exxon emulation lands it in hot water
item.description: Oil major sparks controversy by rejecting shareholder resolution

item.title: European airports face jet fuel shortages within three weeks
item.description: Industry group warns EU that reserves are running low

item.title: Surveying the damage to the Middle East’s energy infrastructure
item.description: A look at the 60-ish facilities hit in the war

item.title: Ukrainian interceptors downed Iran’s drones in Middle East, says Zelenskyy
item.description: Ukraine’s president says military teams dispatched across the Gulf had delivered ‘positive’ results

item.title: Why are small stocks outperforming?
item.description: Plus more on the Strait of Hormuz toll

item.title: North Sea oil prices hit record high as Iran keeps hold over Hormuz
item.description: Washington-Tehran ceasefire agreement fails to stem global energy crunch

item.title: The $20bn+ exodus from private credit
item.description: Groups including Apollo, Ares and Blackstone faced a surge of redemption requests in the first quarter

item.title: UK health officials discuss banning doctors from going on strike
item.description: Wes Streeting has not ruled out policy as desperation grows over walkouts in long-running pay dispute

item.title: Annuities look more attractive as Iran war stirs inflation fears, say advisers
item.description: Benefits of a fixed and stable income set to play more prominent role in retirement planning

item.title: EU boosts imports of Russian gas as Middle East crisis squeezes supplies
item.description: The bloc took 97% of the cargoes from the vast Yamal LNG project in Siberia in the first three months of year

item.title: What Jamie Dimon could learn from the collapse of Northern Rock
item.description: UK bank’s collapse offers a lesson for JPMorgan’s CEO and his idea to overhaul the US’s deposit insurance system

item.title: Canada pushes to join UK-Italy-Japan advanced fighter jet project
item.description: Ottawa lobbying for GCAP admission as ‘observer’ in bid to distance itself from Trump’s US

item.title: It’s time for private credit’s spring cleaning
item.description: Business development companies face the perils of trying to set valuation marks amid market turmoil

item.title: UK must act more swiftly to develop stablecoins
item.description: Country risks missing next phase of mainstream adoption now under way

item.title: What’s stopping you getting your financial act together?
item.description: Many people draw up a solid strategy but fail to implement it

item.title: China’s Alibaba shifts towards revenue over open-source AI
item.description: Move may affect a global developer community that relies on Qwen models from the Chinese company

item.title: Auditor warns on future of UK fine wine investment firm as tariffs bite
item.description: Cult Wines co-founder Tom Gearing says steps to stabilise the business are already bearing fruit

item.title: Why 3i’s ‘Mr No’ said yes to launching discount chain Action in America
item.description: PE firm is betting its star discount retailer will thrive despite slowing growth in Europe

item.title: US-Iran war boosts used electric vehicle sales
item.description: Americans are buying second-hand EVs even as the market for new vehicles slumps

item.title: Bessent called in US bank CEOs to discuss Anthropic model’s cyber risks
item.description: Meeting with US Treasury secretary comes as latest AI system has detected decades-old vulnerabilities

item.title: FT High-Growth Companies Asia-Pacific
item.description: The 2026 ranking of fast growing companies in the Asia-Pacific region. Plus: Indian winemakers face change; Chinese AI experts return from US; the biggest threat to China’s champions; ticket fees donated to charities; Singapore listings remain scarce

item.title: Chinese and Indian airports chase hub status amid Iran disruption
item.description: Countries have so far failed to anchor global traffic compared to Gulf rivals

item.title: Asian start-ups evolve to reshape industries with AI
item.description: Entrepreneurs focus on improving established sectors including logistics, manufacturing and healthcare

item.title: Singapore listings remain scarce despite strong equities performance
item.description: IPO numbers begin to improve, but remain very low compared with Hong Kong

item.title: EU trade deal set to change India’s wine industry
item.description: Domestic producers, already under pressure from overcapacity despite a growing market, are adapting ahead of tariff cuts

item.title: From bingo to Melbourne flower show: Humanitix uses ticket fees as ‘engine for good’
item.description: Australian non-profit takes its business model overseas, expanding to the UK and North America

item.title: The biggest threat to China’s global champions is not Trump
item.description: The most competitive companies are being damaged by an underclass of less productive domestic rivals

item.title: China lures home its top AI talent from Silicon Valley
item.description: Engineers and scientists return for better pay and quality of life as US grows more hostile

item.title: Venezuela passes mining reform law as it seeks private investment
item.description: Corruption and violence in mineral-rich regions likely to pose barrier to foreign companies
